title: Cabinet Console Table
summary: A 72"-wide console with a real face frame and four inset cabinet doors — a proper cabinet build, not a shelf-and-legs console.
description:
Unlike an open-shelf console, this one is a genuine face-frame cabinet: a plywood-backed 1x12 carcass with a center divider, faced with a mitered 1x3 frame, and closed up with four inset doors. Inset doors are the detail that separates a nice piece from an average one, and they're also the detail most likely to go wrong — get the door sizing and gap consistent and it reads as furniture; get it inconsistent and every uneven gap draws the eye.

Build and attach the face frame before the back goes on, not after — you need to be able to get a drill inside to drive the pocket screws, and a plywood back closes that access off completely. Size each door 1/4" less than its opening in both width and height, which nets an even 1/8" gap around all four sides once it's hung — measure your actual openings before cutting the doors, since real-world lumber width rarely matches a plan exactly.

tools.note: For the doors and any rip cuts.

materials.name: Pine, 1x12, 8 ft
materials.note: Top, sides, center divider, bottom shelf, shelves.

cutList.note: Cut to fit each side of the base.
Size to your actual openings — 1/4" less in width and height for an even 1/8" gap all around.

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Assemble the top, sides, center divider, and bottom shelf into a box with pocket holes and glue. If you're adding the face frame with pocket holes hidden on the inside, drill those holes now, before assembly, with the 3/4" setting facing forward — you want the face frame on before the back closes off drill access.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame separately with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, then attach it to the front of the carcass with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Nail the plywood back panel on with 3/4" brad nails, checking the carcass for square right before it goes on — the back is your last chance to correct it.

steps.title: Add interior shelving
steps.body: Install the interior shelves either on shelf pins for adjustability, or fixed in place with pocket holes on their undersides.

steps.title: Add base trim
steps.body: Attach the base moulding around the bottom of the cabinet, mitered at the corners.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Measure your four door openings directly rather than trusting the plan numbers — build each door 1/4" less in width and height than its opening, which gives an even 1/8" gap on all sides once hinged. Install the doors so the gaps read consistent, and add a magnetic catch if your hinge style doesn't hold the door closed on its own.

images.alt: A wide cabinet console table with a face frame and four inset doors
